components.name: Accordion
components.description: A single collapsible item with trigger label, chevron icon, and expandable content area. Bottom border separates stacked items (hidden on last via End Item). Built on @radix-ui/react-accordion.

children.name: Text
children.textContent: Yes. It adheres to the WAI-ARIA design pattern.

examples.name: States
examples.description: Default, hover (underline on trigger label), focus (ring), and disabled (50% opacity).

examples.name: Open / Closed
examples.description: Closed shows only the trigger; open reveals the content below with the chevron rotated.

examples.name: End Item
examples.description: End Item=True removes the bottom border — use for the last item in a stacked group.

props.name: type
props.description: Selection mode — required. single: one item open; multiple: several open simultaneously

props.name: collapsible
props.description: Allow all items to be closed (single mode only)

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: Uncontrolled: item(s) open by default on mount

props.name: value
props.description: Controlled open item(s) — pair with onValueChange

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disable all triggers in the accordion

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS classes on the root element

bestPractices.do: Use type='single' when sections are mutually exclusive — only one answer/section is relevant at a time.
bestPractices.dont: Default to type='multiple' — it causes information overload and users can't find what they need.

bestPractices.do: Add collapsible={true} on single-mode accordions so users can close all panels when done.
bestPractices.dont: Leave collapsible={false} as it traps users with content open they can't dismiss.

bestPractices.do: Keep trigger labels short and scannable — users read triggers to decide whether to expand.
bestPractices.dont: Put critical, must-read content inside collapsed sections — users may never see it.

keyboard.action: Move focus to the next accordion trigger
Move focus to the previous accordion trigger
Toggle the focused section open or closed
Move focus to the next trigger (wraps to first)
Move focus to the previous trigger (wraps to last)
Move focus to the first trigger
Move focus to the last trigger

notes: AccordionTrigger renders as a <button> inside an <h3> — the heading level aids page structure for screen readers.
Each trigger has aria-expanded reflecting its open/closed state, and aria-controls pointing to the content panel.
Content panels have role='region' and aria-labelledby pointing back to their trigger.
Disabled items have pointer-events-none and opacity-50 — still present in the DOM and announced as disabled.

related.name: Collapsible
related.desc: Single toggle section — use when you only need one expandable area rather than a stacked list.

related.name: Tabs
related.desc: Horizontal section switcher — use when sections are mutually exclusive and space is limited.

related.name: Card
related.desc: Always-visible content container — use when content should not be hidden behind an interaction.
